title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (May 13)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: A Colonial home in Acton at 96 Newtown Road sold for $1,790,000 on a 43,585-square-foot lot.
A two-family Cape Cod in Bellingham at 1099 S Main St. sold for $650,000, featuring five bedrooms.
A Boston high-rise condo at 1 Dalton St. #2703 sold for $4,900,000, comprising 2,226 square feet.

title: North Adams man convicted, sentenced for murdering his wife in 2022
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Luis Rosado, 53, was convicted of first-degree murder for the 2022 stabbing death of his wife, Jillian Tatro.
Rosado was sentenced to life in prison without parole after the verdict in the Berkshire County Court.
Tatro’s body was found by her son in her North Adams apartment, with multiple stab and slash wounds.

title: State officials release 4 submissions to immigration agent misconduct portal launched in March
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: State officials released four reports of misconduct from an ICE portal opened in March, detailing alleged unlawful agent activities.
Reports include incidents of interrogation at Logan Airport, armed arrests in Cohasset, and agent presence near Needham High School.
Governor Healey and AG Campbell's offices didn't disclose the total submissions received, citing ongoing reviews with possible public releases.

title: Weighing pros, cons of Celtics trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Celtics are considering a trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo to boost their championship chances with Jayson Tatum.
Trading for Giannis may require sending Jaylen Brown to the Bucks, affecting the Celtics' current roster depth.
Giannis's age and potential decline are concerns, but he could elevate Boston's contention during Tatum's peak years.

title: Harvard College faculty to vote on limiting number of As for students
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Harvard faculty voting on a proposal to cap As at 20% plus four per class to combat grade inflation.
More than 60% of Harvard grades are currently As, a significant increase from 24% in 2005.
Critics argue the cap pits students against each other and raises ethical concerns in subjective grading.

title: Truck driver dies days after crash on North Attleborough highway
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Julio Flores from Pawtucket, Rhode Island, died from injuries sustained in a crash on I-95 South in North Attleborough.
The crash involved a 2005 Freightliner M2 truck and a 2014 Volkswagen Jetta with four occupants pinned against a guardrail.
The accident is under investigation by State Police, and no updates have been given about the Jetta's occupants' conditions.

title: Sports Hub’s Mike Felger shares wild Red Sox trade rumor involving Mike Trout
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Mike Felger shared a rumor on 98.5 The Sports Hub about the Red Sox interest in trading for Mike Trout.
Trout, a three-time MVP, is having a strong season but has struggled with injuries and carries a large contract.
If traded, Trout could be swapped for Red Sox players Jarren Duran and Brayan Bello, per Felger's source.

title: Romeo Doubs on learning Josh McDaniels’ offense: ‘I can already see how he thinks’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Romeo Doubs transitions from Packers to Patriots, learning Josh McDaniels' offensive strategies for the 2026 NFL season.
Doubs appreciates quarterback Drake Maye's energy and aims to adapt to various roles in New England Patriots' system.
Doubs targets a successful Patriots season influenced by winning culture and potential additions like AJ Brown.

title: DA: Mass. man walked into police station, told officers he killed his father
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: David Boucher, 48, charged with first degree murder after allegedly confessing to killing his father in North Adams.
Victim, David A. Boucher, found stabbed at a Walnut Street home, reportedly murdered a week before confession.
Boucher arraigned, pleaded not guilty, held without bail; pretrial hearing set for June 12, investigation ongoing.

title: Worcester man, 62, killed by vehicle while crossing street, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: James McGuire, 62, was fatally struck by a vehicle near 137 Hope Ave. in Worcester on Sunday night.
McGuire was crossing the street when he was hit and later died at the hospital, police reported.
Worcester police are investigating the incident; details about the driver or potential charges are not yet released.
